prompt:
Ops tooling request, PowerShell. Our ONTAP snapshot sprawl is out of hand — thousands of snapshots per volume, and the last person who 'cleaned up' deleted a week of SnapMirror baselines with a wildcard. I want a proper PowerShell module, OntapSnapshots, that talks to the cluster-management `/api` endpoint, pages through the snapshot inventory the way the API actually paginates, picks deletion candidates conservatively, and deletes them one at a time through the asynchronous job flow so we get a per-snapshot verdict. test_snapshot_pages.ps1 is the acceptance contract — `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_snapshot_pages.ps1` runs everything against a loopback mock cluster: no real filer, dummy credentials only, and no Start-Sleep in the module (pacing comes in as a scriptblock).

Do the research first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official NetApp ONTAP REST API documentation — at minimum the getting-started page (the `/api` base path, basic auth, the HAL+JSON error object, fields/max_records/order_by query parameters, and following `_links.next.href` for pagination), the GET and DELETE references for /api/storage/volumes/{volume.uuid}/snapshots (record fields including expiry_time/snapmirror_label/owners, and the 202-plus-job DELETE), and the snapshots endpoint overview. Only first-party NetApp documentation (docs.netapp.com, NetApp's official NetAppDocs docs source on GitHub, or library.netapp.com) counts as a source. The pinned wire contract is in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json — protected, same as the test file; if the current official docs disagree with anything pinned there, call that out in your summary instead of editing protected files. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final response.

Contract the tests import (module file OntapSnapshots.psm1 at the repo root; split the implementation across a few dot-sourced files — transport, paging, retention, jobs — your layout, 3+ files total, Set-StrictMode Latest everywhere):
- New-OntapSession -BaseUri <string> -Credential <pscredential> -> object with BaseUri (trailing slash trimmed) and AuthHeader (the documented Basic header). No plaintext Password property, and no error message anywhere may contain the password.
- Get-OntapSnapshotInventory -Session -VolumeUuid -Fields <string[]> -MaxRecords <int> -OrderBy <string> -> object with Records (server order preserved across pages), Pages, NumRecords. First request builds fields (comma-joined), max_records, order_by (URL-encoded); every later page follows `_links.next.href` byte-for-byte against the same host — never rebuild it or append parameters; stop when there is no next link.
- Select-OntapSnapshotsForDeletion -Snapshots -AsOf <datetime> [-SnapmirrorLabel <string>] -> only snapshots whose expiry_time exists and is at or before AsOf, with no owners (absent or empty), and matching the label case-sensitively when given; ordered by create_time then name. Snapshots legitimately lack expiry_time/snapmirror_label — strict mode must not blow up on them.
- Remove-OntapSnapshots -Session -VolumeUuid -Snapshots -PollLimit <int> -Sleep <scriptblock> -> one result object per input snapshot (Uuid, Name, Deleted, ErrorCode, ErrorMessage), in order. Each DELETE uses return_timeout=0, expects 202 with a job, and polls the job's exact `_links.self.href` — Sleep is invoked with 1 (second) between polls, PollLimit bounds polls per snapshot and exhausting it reports a 'poll limit' failure for that snapshot. A failed job (e.g. the documented locked-snapshot error) is recorded on its result and the run continues; an HTTP-level HAL error on the DELETE itself (e.g. 403) throws immediately with the ONTAP code and message and stops the run.

test_snapshot_pages.ps1 and everything under docs/ are protected — leave them alone. Finish by running the acceptance command and citing the official doc URLs you used.

research.product: NetApp ONTAP REST API (ONTAP 9.18.1 reference; contract stable since 9.6)
research.interface: ONTAP REST API /api/storage/volumes/{volume.uuid}/snapshots with HAL pagination and /api/cluster/jobs

official_sources.title: Getting started with the ONTAP REST API (NetApp official docs source for docs.netapp.com/us-en/ontap-restapi/getting_started_with_the_ontap_rest_api.html)
official_sources.used_for: cluster-management /api base path, HTTP basic authentication, HAL+JSON error object {error:{message,code,target}}, collection shape {records,num_records,_links} and pagination by following _links.next.href, query parameters fields/max_records/order_by/return_timeout

official_sources.title: Retrieve volume snapshots (GET /api/storage/volumes/{volume.uuid}/snapshots)
official_sources.used_for: snapshot collection query parameters (fields, max_records, order_by with [asc|desc], name/create_time/expiry_time/snapmirror_label filters) and snapshot record fields uuid, name, create_time, expiry_time, snapmirror_label, size, comment, owners, state

official_sources.title: Delete a volume snapshot (DELETE /api/storage/volumes/{volume.uuid}/snapshots/{uuid})
official_sources.used_for: DELETE path parameters volume.uuid and uuid, return_timeout default 0 giving HTTP 202 with a job object {job:{uuid,_links.self.href}}, and documented snapshot deletion error codes including 1638543 (snapshot has an owner) and 1638555 (snapshot has not expired or is locked)

official_sources.title: Storage volumes snapshots endpoint overview
official_sources.used_for: endpoint base path /api/storage/volumes/{volume.uuid}/snapshots, fields-based attribute selection, and confirmation that snapshot deletion is an asynchronous 202-plus-job operation

research.language_rationale: PowerShell is a first-class ONTAP operations surface: NetApp ships the official NetApp.ONTAP PowerShell toolkit, and storage/retention runbooks in Windows shops are PowerShell. The task drives the documented raw REST contract (the same one the toolkit wraps) with pwsh built-ins so acceptance stays hermetic and dependency-free.
